page: Microsoft Azure E4ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 4 dedicated virtual CPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type includes 236 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. It does not include any integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ads_v7 offers a high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of larger multi-core instances. The combination of dedicated AMD vCPUs, a 1:8 CPU-to-RAM ratio, and local temporary storage makes this instance suitable for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring moderate compute power but substantial memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure E4ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an AMD processor, it features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 236 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that do not require massive parallel compute resources. The E4ads_v7 is designed for workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caches, data processing tasks, and general enterprise applications requiring a substantial memory footprint relative to the virtual CPU count.
